headline_findings: CRITICAL: Choice-marker normalization is broken systemically across exam-type pages (p043, p086, p204, p341, p416). Three distinct failure modes: (1) Japanese Katakana ア/イ/ウ/エ leaked into zh output (p204 Q1-3, p416 Q2, Q5; partial in others); (2) CJK ordinals 甲乙丙丁 substituted for A/B/C/D in zh (p043 Q2-4, p086 Q2-5, p341 Q0, p416 Q3); (3) lowercase en a./b./c./d. on p416 Q0-1 (confirms R9-A's prior flag).
Mixed-style WITHIN single question observed multiple times (p043 Q1, p086 Q1, p204 Q1, p341 Q1, p416 Q1, p416 Q4) — labeling inconsistent across the four choices of one question.
Translation content itself (stems, choice bodies, term definitions) is faithful and fluent throughout — defects are surface/normalization, not semantic.
p540 (terms+figures) and p044 (single SDGs question) are fully clean.
p136 has zero leaves due to upstream (structurer) dropout — translation track is vacuously PASS but the page silently missing from release; flagged for orchestrator.
release_impact_summary: 6 of 8 pages release-impacting (p043, p086, p204, p341, p416 for translation; p136 for upstream dropout). Choice-marker normalization defect is a release-blocker for the trilingual exam UX — answer key cannot resolve when zh/en labels disagree with jp ア/イ/ウ/エ.
